# Stop Sending Emails to the Wrong Inboxes #coldemail #mistake
**Channel:** Nick Abraham
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-CY-qCLDKZQ
**Date:** 2026-04-15
**Content source:** auto-generated transcript

## Summary
Nick Abraham discusses the importance of segmenting your cold email campaigns by contact type, specifically highlighting a third campaign strategy for "public emails" (generic company inboxes like info@, hello@, contact@, support@, sales@). Despite initial skepticism, public emails outperform expectations in SMB and local business markets when properly segmented and monitored.

## Key Points & Strategies
- **Segment by contact type:** Create separate campaigns for public/generic company emails rather than lumping them into your main outreach
- **Public emails perform better in SMB/local markets:** Generic inboxes like info@, hello@, contact@, support@, sales@ show strong response rates in smaller business segments
- **Adjust messaging for generic contacts:** Use a generic opener like "Hey, this is the best email I could find for you, and I wanted to reach out because..." when you don't have a prospect's first name
- **Monitor performance separately:** Track public email campaign metrics independently to gather industry-specific data for your clients
- **Enterprise caveat:** This strategy applies primarily to SMB/mid-market; skip it if targeting enterprise SaaS

## Workflows & Tools Discussed
- **Apollo** - Database for lead research and contact discovery
- **Prospect Bio** - Alternative contact database
- **List Kit** - Alternative contact database
- Multi-campaign structure with segmented tracking

## Use Cases Covered
- Local businesses and SMBs (primary use case)
- Mid-market companies
- Leads not found in standard contact databases

## Quotes Worth Noting
> "They're really receptive in these inboxes and they perform better."

> "You want to put this into its own campaign as well, just so you can monitor how the public emails do in that industry and for that offer and that segment that you're hitting because it's really good data you can give back to your clients."
